holes.content: Decoupling action execution from context management makes the Context Manager reusable across different Actors and tasks, and allows context-management performance to be analyzed and improved independently of Actor capability.
holes.label: decoupling_enables_reuse

holes.content: Always-on incremental summarization preserves complete semantic information from the most recent interaction *before* any abstraction or omission accumulates across multiple steps, yielding a compact yet semantically faithful representation of the agent's past experience.
holes.label: incremental_preserves_evidence

holes.content: Reflection-driven reorganization enables the agent to repair degraded context: incorrect early assumptions, outdated information, or misplaced emphasis can be rewritten, merged, or removed — modifications that incremental summarization alone cannot perform because it never revises previously stored content.
holes.label: reflection_enables_repair

holes.content:
Table 1 reports Pass@1 / Pass@3 accuracies for ReAct, ReSum, and ARC across five actor models and five benchmarks. Selected entries:

**Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct** (Pass@1):
- HotpotQA: ReAct 65.5, ReSum 68.6, ARC 71.3
- GAIA: ReAct 28.5, ReSum 27.3, ARC 34.9
- xBench-DS: ReAct 33.3, ReSum 36.3, ARC 40.7
- BrowseComp: ReAct 1.0, ReSum 1.2, ARC 3.0
- BrowseComp-ZH: ReAct 7.1, ReSum 7.4, ARC 18.0 (+10.9 vs ReAct, +10.6 vs ReSum)

**Qwen3-14B** (Pass@1): GAIA ReAct 26.5 / ReSum 31.3 / ARC 41.0; xBench-DS ReAct 32.6 / ReSum 32.0 / ARC 46.0; BrowseComp-ZH ReAct 8.5 / ReSum 10.0 / ARC 17.9.

**DeepSeek-v3.2** (Pass@1): GAIA ReAct 63.7 / ReSum 67.1 / ARC 69.1; BrowseComp ReAct 16.8 / ReSum 22.2 / ARC 26.6; BrowseComp-ZH ReAct 37.7 / ReSum 45.7 / ARC 51.7.

Across every reported actor, ARC's Pass@1 exceeds both ReAct and ReSum on the harder search-heavy settings (GAIA, xBench-DS, BrowseComp, BrowseComp-ZH).
holes.label: table1_observation

holes.content:
Table 2 (averaged Pass@1 over GAIA / xBench-DS / BrowseComp-ZH; same Qwen2.5-32B Actor):

| Variant | GAIA | xBench-DS | BrowseComp-ZH |
|---|---|---|---|
| Summary | 32.1 | 38.4 | 14.4 |
| Summary + Checklist | 33.0 | 37.6 | 15.0 |
| Reflection + Checklist-Only | 26.2 | 34.3 | 6.9 |
| ARC (Full) | 34.9 | 40.7 | 18.0 |

ARC (Full) is best on every benchmark. Summary+Checklist gives only marginal/inconsistent gains over Summary. The Reflection+Checklist-Only variant — which forbids memory revision — is *worse* than the Summary baseline on every benchmark, especially BrowseComp-ZH (6.9 vs 14.4).
holes.label: table2_observation

holes.content:
Table 3 (Pass@1 with fixed Actor, varying CM):

| CM | HotpotQA | GAIA | xBench-DS |
|---|---|---|---|
| Qwen3-32B | 72.1 | 35.1 | 39.0 |
| GPT-OSS-120B | 74.6 | 34.9 | 40.7 |
| DeepSeek-v3.2 | 75.4 | 48.4 | 52.0 |
| ARC-CM (trained, Qwen3-14B base) | 76.1 | 42.6 | 46.3 |

ARC-CM (~14B) outperforms the much larger GPT-OSS-120B on every benchmark (76.1 vs 74.6, 42.6 vs 34.9, 46.3 vs 40.7), and outperforms Qwen3-32B as well, while being substantially smaller. DeepSeek-v3.2 (a larger, stronger general model) still beats ARC-CM on GAIA and xBench-DS.
holes.label: table3_observation

holes.content:
Table 4 (average accuracy over GAIA, xBench-DS, BrowseComp):

| Strategy | Setting | Accuracy (%) |
|---|---|---|
| Step-by-step | Every turn | 31.2 |
| Delayed | Every 3 turns | 26.5 |
| Delayed | Every 5 turns | 24.5 |
| Budget-triggered | 8k tokens | 27.1 |
| Budget-triggered | 16k tokens | 24.4 |
| Budget-triggered | 32k tokens | 24.6 |

Per-turn step-by-step management (31.2) strictly beats every delayed and every budget-triggered alternative.
holes.label: table4_observation
